key_insights.insight: The loss of a male role model is a key predictor of struggles for young boys, leading to increased incarceration, criminal activity, and self-harm.
key_insights.quote: Boys, once they lose a male role model, become much more likely to be incarcerated, engage in criminal activity, harm themselves.

key_insights.insight: Boys are often weaker mentally and emotionally than girls, requiring more support and guidance during childhood.
key_insights.quote: It ends up that while being physically stronger, boys are emotionally and mentally much weaker.

key_insights.insight: Educational environments and practices often favor female learning styles, potentially disadvantaging boys.
key_insights.quote: Sit still, be a pleaser, be organized, raise your hand. You basically just described a girl.

key_insights.insight: Economic policies have inadvertently favored the top 1% of high achievers, leaving many unremarkable individuals with limited opportunities.
key_insights.quote: Our economic policies have basically said that school and college is meant to identify a super class of 1%ers that we're gonna try and turn into billionaires. Instead of figuring out the infrastructure and the programs to ensure the bottom 90 have a shot at being in the top 10.

key_insights.insight: Emotional intelligence has become a vital currency in dating, but many men were raised to prioritize being a provider, not an emotional support.
key_insights.quote: Emotional intelligence is the new currency in dating. But these guys were raised not to be emotionally intelligent, not to give emotional support, but to be a provider.

key_insights.insight: Online dating has created a winner-take-most environment that negatively impacts average-looking men and negatively affects women by increasing competition.
key_insights.quote: On Tinder, a man of average attractiveness has to swipe right 200 times to get one coffee and then four of those five coffees will ghost him...What does that tell that guy? Women don't value me.

key_insights.insight: Men are expected to approach and lead in dating but now face increased scrutiny and fear of being perceived as creepy in a post-MeToo era.
key_insights.quote: Men are expected to lead and to approach, but I truly feel like in a post-MeToo era, it's much more confusing.

key_insights.insight: There's a growing trend in men finding father figures online but these are not the ones society wants them to have.
key_insights.quote: Men are finding these father figures, but they're finding them online and they're not the father figures that I would choose for the majority of men.

actionable_advice.advice: Men should join men's groups for support and guidance from their peers.
actionable_advice.how_to: Find or create a group that meets regularly where members can openly discuss their issues and hold each other accountable.

actionable_advice.advice: Women should consider lowering height filters on dating apps and be open to second dates to find potentially compatible partners.
actionable_advice.how_to: Adjust height filter on dating apps to include a wider range of men; commit to giving a second date to someone even if the initial connection isn't immediately strong.

actionable_advice.advice: Delay formal education for boys by one year to allow their brains to develop at a more similar rate to girls.
actionable_advice.how_to: Consider starting kindergarten at age six instead of five to put boys on a more equal playing field for critical moments of development.

actionable_advice.advice: Men need to build emotional intelligence and willingness to express emotions.
actionable_advice.how_to: Men should be willing to reach out when struggling and turn to someone instead of continuing to struggle alone.

actionable_advice.advice: Modulate and potentially eliminate porn consumption, particularly among young men.
actionable_advice.how_to: Aim to put the majority of their sexual energy into their romantic partner. If they cannot, go no-fap for a period of time to reset the body.

actionable_advice.advice: Young men should reinvest their time, energy, and capital into high-ROI areas.
actionable_advice.how_to: Spend 8-12 hours working out to get stronger, making money, and involving themselves in the company of strangers (church groups, nonprofits, etc.)

actionable_advice.advice: Men need to be willing to endure rejection. In a continued effort to step outside of comfort zones, aim to ask at least one new person out each week in person.
actionable_advice.how_to: To conquer the fear of rejection, make it a goal to ask out a stranger, even if you know they will say no, then celebrating that you endured the no.

memorable_stories: An anonymous teacher in Germany reports children, always boys, having a destructive energy.
The speaker's friend had to start meeting with all men group to become less funny, so that the humor wasn't a distraction from truly going deep.
A former student started going to therapy after a bad review about his movie to unpack the negative emotions that he had never been able to address.

A CEO was ashamed when his son was born because he was broken was a terrible provider and the feelings of shame was so overwhelming. It wasn't long after that he began helping other men as an action to counteract.
A man met the mother of his kids at a pool, and the baby's middle name became Raleigh after the hotel they met.
A man has a friend go through a bankruptcy and never felt more worthless.
contrarian_claims: Delaying kindergarten for boys would help them be more ready.
There is no such thing as toxic masculinity.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Masculinity Debate: Are Dating Apps Creating A Generation Of Incels?! Lonely Men Are More Dangerous Than Ever!
